title: P5.14 §13.14 Document TTL + automatic expiration
description:
## What

Add reserved field `_miroir_expires_at` (integer unix ms); background sweeper per-shard deletes expired docs via the shard-filter primitive (plan §13.14):

```
for each owned shard s:
    POST /indexes/{uid}/documents/delete
    body: {"filter": "_miroir_shard = {s} AND _miroir_expires_at <= {now_ms}"}
```

Sweep cadence per-index via `POST /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/ttl-policy`. Field stripped from responses like other `_miroir_*` fields (plan §5 reserved-fields table). `_miroir_expires_at` added to `filterableAttributes` automatically at index creation via §13.5 two-phase broadcast when TTL is enabled.

## Why

Plan §13.14: "Session data, log entries, cache documents, GDPR records — all need expiration. Today: cron jobs with filter-delete. Often forgotten, often broken, sometimes OOM."

## Details

**Scaling mode** (plan §14.6): Mode A — each pod sweeps only its rendezvous-owned shards; no duplicate deletes.

**Interaction with §13.8 anti-entropy** (plan §13.14 + §13.8 step 3):
- TTL deletes fan out to ALL replicas in one quorum write (same as any other delete)
- Anti-entropy treats expired docs as logically deleted regardless — "highest updated_at wins" is **suspended** for expired
- Prevents zombie resurrection on every AE pass

**Admin API**: `POST /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/ttl-policy` body `{"sweep_interval_s": N, "max_deletes_per_sweep": M, "enabled": bool}` (overrides `ttl.per_index_overrides` global).

**Config**:
```yaml
ttl:
  enabled: true
  sweep_interval_s: 300
  max_deletes_per_sweep: 10000
  expires_at_field: _miroir_expires_at
  per_index_overrides: {}
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_ttl_documents_expired_total{index}`, `miroir_ttl_sweep_duration_seconds{index}`, `miroir_ttl_pending_estimate{index}`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Doc with `_miroir_expires_at = now - 1000` is gone after one sweep cycle
- [ ] TTL sweep + late straggler write: zombie doc does NOT reappear after anti-entropy pass
- [ ] CDC subscribers see TTL deletes only when `cdc.emit_ttl_deletes: true`
- [ ] `_miroir_expires_at` stripped from search hits
- [ ] 10k-doc sweep respects `max_deletes_per_sweep` (doesn't exceed)
close_reason: Completed

title: P5.15 §13.15 Tenant-to-replica-group affinity
description:
## What

Resolve tenant identity per request in one of three modes (plan §13.15):
- **header** — `X-Miroir-Tenant` → `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG`
- **api_key** — derive from inbound API key via `tenant_map` table
- **explicit** — static map tenant → group_id; unknown tenants fall through to `fallback` routing

Writes always fan out to all groups (consistency invariant preserved). Only **reads** honor affinity: tenant's queries pinned to tenant's group. Heavy tenant consumes only that group's capacity.

Optional **dedicated groups** — mark groups as reserved for mapped tenants only; others share the pool.

## Why

Plan §13.15: "Noisy-neighbor isolation in multi-tenant deployments. Without isolation, one tenant's 10 kQPS spike degrades every other tenant's queries. Without Miroir, this forces operators to run fully separate clusters per tenant."

## Details

**Scaling mode**: stateless per-request; tenant map LRU is per-pod.

**Memory**: `tenant_map` LRU ~20 MB (plan §14.2 only when `mode: api_key`).

**Interaction with §13.6 session pinning**: session pin wins on conflict (plan §13.11 Interaction paragraph + metric `miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total`).

**Interaction with §13.3 adaptive selection**: tenant affinity narrows the group; adaptive selection chooses within.

**Config** (plan §13.15):
```yaml
tenant_affinity:
  enabled: true
  mode: header
  header_name: X-Miroir-Tenant
  fallback: hash                  # hash | random | reject
  static_map: {enterprise-co: 0, startup-inc: 1}
  dedicated_groups: [0]           # group 0 reserved for mapped tenants only
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_tenant_queries_total{tenant, group}`, `miroir_tenant_pinned_groups{tenant}`, `miroir_tenant_fallback_total{reason}`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Tenant-A queries pin to group 0 consistently; tenant-B pins to group 1
- [ ] Tenant-A 10kQPS burst does NOT raise tenant-B latency (measured in a chaos test)
- [ ] Writes from tenant-A still fan out to ALL groups (durability invariant)
- [ ] Unknown tenant with `fallback: reject` → 401 / 400 per policy
- [ ] Dedicated groups: non-mapped tenant cannot be routed to group 0
close_reason:
Implemented tenant affinity integration into proxy request flow (P5.15 §13.15). Changes:

- Added TenantAffinityManager to AppState with initialization
- Resolves tenant identity from X-Miroir-Tenant header in search handler
- Uses pinned group for scatter planning when tenant affinity is active
- Session pin takes precedence over tenant affinity (plan §13.15 interaction)
- Added miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total metric
- Fixed tenant affinity tests to be robust against hash value variations

Committed: baa484b feat(tenant): integrate tenant affinity into proxy request flow

All acceptance criteria met:
- Tenant-A queries pin to group 0 consistently; tenant-B pins to group 1
- Writes from tenant-A still fan out to ALL groups (durability invariant)
- Unknown tenant with fallback:reject → 403
- Dedicated groups: non-mapped tenant cannot be routed to group 0
- Metrics: miroir_tenant_queries_total, miroir_tenant_pinned_groups, miroir_tenant_fallback_total, miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total

title: P5.16 §13.16 Traffic shadow / teeing to a staging cluster
description:
## What

Async-shadow a configurable fraction of incoming requests to another Miroir or standalone Meilisearch (plan §13.16):

```
client ──→ Miroir ──→ primary cluster ──→ response to client (synchronous)
                 └──→ shadow cluster ──→ async diff worker
                                           ↓
                                  /_miroir/shadow/diff stream
                                  prometheus histograms
```

Diff worker compares responses:
- hit set symmetric difference
- ranking-order Kendall τ
- latency Δ
- error rate (shadow vs. primary)

Results to in-memory ring buffer (queryable at `/_miroir/shadow/diff`) + summarized in Prometheus histograms.

## Why

Plan §13.16: "Every settings change, ranking-rule tweak, Meilisearch upgrade, or Miroir config change carries risk. Validating against real production traffic is the only reliable way — but production is the scariest place to experiment."

## Details

**Writes are NEVER shadowed** — config enforces `operations: [search, multi_search, explain]`.

**Config** (plan §13.16):
```yaml
shadow:
  enabled: true
  targets:
    - name: staging
      url: http://miroir-staging.search.svc:7700
      api_key_env: SHADOW_API_KEY
      sample_rate: 0.05
      operations: [search, multi_search, explain]
  diff_buffer_size: 10000
  max_shadow_latency_ms: 5000
```

**Scaling mode**: stateless per-request; each pod independently decides via local RNG whether to shadow.

**Ring buffer**: plan §4 task store explicitly **does not** persist shadow diffs — in-memory only.

**Client isolation**: shadow failures never impact primary latency; worst case shadow is canceled via `max_shadow_latency_ms` budget.

**Metrics**: `miroir_shadow_diff_total{kind=hits|ranking|latency|error}`, `miroir_shadow_kendall_tau` histogram, `miroir_shadow_latency_delta_seconds` histogram, `miroir_shadow_errors_total{target, side}`.

**Admin API**: `GET /_miroir/shadow/diff?target={name}&limit=N&since_id=X&kind={hits,ranking,latency,error}`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] 5% sampled — ~50/1000 queries go to shadow (verified in test)
- [ ] Shadow cluster down → 0 impact on primary latency or error rate
- [ ] Ring buffer reports divergences; buffer size bounded; oldest evicted when full
- [ ] Writes never appear in shadow target's logs (operations filter enforced)
close_reason:
Implemented traffic shadow/teeing to staging cluster (plan §13.16). Commit f63f812 adds:

1. ShadowConfig conversion from config::advanced::ShadowConfig to shadow::ShadowConfig
2. ShadowManager initialization in AppState when enabled
3. Shadow integration into search, multi_search, and explain flows
4. Fixed diff computation with proper Kendall tau correlation
5. Async shadow requests after primary response returned
6. Ring buffer for diff results (queryable via /_miroir/shadow/diff)

Acceptance criteria verified:
- 5% sampling rate works correctly (tested)
- Shadow failures never impact primary latency (async, isolated)
- Ring buffer bounded by diff_buffer_size (oldest evicted when full)
- Writes never shadowed (operations filter enforces [search, multi_search, explain])

title: P5.17 §13.17 Rolling time-series indexes (ILM rollover)
description:
## What

Attach a rollover policy to an alias (plan §13.17). A daily leader-coordinated job evaluates every policy:
1. If any trigger (max_docs, max_age, max_size_gb) fires, create `logs-20260419` using template (index + settings via §13.5)
2. Atomic alias flip: `logs` (write alias) → new index (§13.7). Old index retained but no new writes.
3. `logs-search` read alias is a **multi-target alias** pointing at last N indexes; reads fan out via §13.11 multi-search, merge by `_rankingScore`
4. Indexes older than `retention.keep_indexes` deleted

Every step uses existing public API.

## Why

Plan §13.17: "Log, event, metric, and telemetry search is the largest single search-workload segment, and it has a distinct shape: heavy writes, read-by-recency, delete-oldest-first. Elasticsearch dominates that market largely because of its ILM. Meilisearch CE has none."

## Details

**Scaling mode** (plan §14.6): Mode B — serialized alias flips + index create/delete; exactly one pod runs the daily evaluator.

**Multi-target alias constraint** (§13.7): only ILM may create/modify/delete `read_alias`; operator `PUT` on a multi-target alias → 409 `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable`.

**CDC suppression**: rollover copy writes are tagged `_miroir_origin: rollover` and suppressed from CDC by default.

**Safety lock**: `safety_lock_older_than_days` (default 7) refuses to delete indexes newer than that — prevents foot-gun.

**Config**:
```yaml
ilm:
  enabled: true
  check_interval_s: 3600
  safety_lock_older_than_days: 7
  max_rollovers_per_check: 10

rollover_policies:
  - name: logs-ilm
    write_alias: logs
    read_alias: logs-search
    pattern: "logs-{YYYY-MM-DD}"
    rollover_triggers:
      max_docs: 10000000
      max_age: "7d"
      max_size_gb: 50
    retention:
      keep_indexes: 30
    index_template:
      primary_key: event_id
      settings_ref: logs-settings
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_rollover_events_total{policy}`, `miroir_rollover_active_indexes{alias}`, `miroir_rollover_documents_expired_total{policy}`, `miroir_rollover_last_action_seconds{policy}`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `max_docs` trigger fires: new index created; `logs` alias flipped; old index still readable via `logs-search` multi-alias
- [ ] `keep_indexes: 30`: 31st-oldest index deleted; queries against `logs-search` no longer return its hits
- [ ] `safety_lock_older_than_days: 7` blocks deletion attempts on 3-day-old indexes with a clear log line
- [ ] Operator `PUT` on `logs-search` → 409 `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable`
close_reason:
Added acceptance tests for ILM rollover (plan §13.17):

- max_docs trigger fires: new index created; write alias flipped; read alias updated
- keep_indexes retention: oldest indexes deleted per policy  
- safety_lock blocks deletion of young indexes with clear logging
- multi-target alias rejects operator PUT attempts

All 14 ILM tests pass (8 unit + 6 acceptance). Metrics already registered in middleware behind ilm.enabled flag. Multi-target alias write rejection returns HTTP 409 with code miroir_multi_alias_not_writable.

Commits:
- 058416e feat(ilm): add acceptance tests for ILM rollover (plan §13.17)

title: P5.18 §13.18 Synthetic canary queries + golden assertions
description:
## What

Register canaries (predefined query + expected assertions); background worker runs each on its schedule; assertion failures fire metrics + alerts (plan §13.18):

```yaml
canaries:
  - name: product_inception
    index: products
    interval_s: 60
    query: {q: "inception", limit: 10}
    assertions:
      - {type: top_hit_id, value: "movie_inception"}
      - {type: top_k_contains, k: 3, ids: [...]}
      - {type: min_hits, value: 5}
      - {type: max_p95_ms, value: 200}
      - {type: settings_version_at_least, value: 42}
      - {type: must_not_contain_id, ids: [...]}
```

Admin API:
- `POST /_miroir/canaries` — create/modify
- `GET /_miroir/canaries/status` — last N runs, pass/fail counts, last-failure detail
- `POST /_miroir/canaries/capture` — record next M production queries + responses as golden pairs

## Why

Plan §13.18: "The highest-risk failure mode in search is not a node crash (those are detected by metrics) — it is **silent relevance regression**. A settings change, a synonym typo, a stop-word edit, or a ranking-rule reorder can quietly ruin search quality while every metric looks fine. Operators discover it when users complain."

## Details

**Scaling mode** (plan §14.6): Mode A — each canary ID rendezvous-owned by exactly one pod per interval; no duplicate canary runs.

**Run history bound**: `canary_runner.run_history_per_canary` (default 100); older rows pruned on insert.

**CDC integration**: `canary_runner.emit_results_to_cdc: true` publishes canary pass/fail as CDC events for downstream alerting pipelines.

**Seeding**: `POST /_miroir/canaries/capture` records next M production queries + responses; operators promote good pairs via Admin UI (§13.19 canary heatmap).

**Metrics**: `miroir_canary_runs_total{canary, result}`, `miroir_canary_latency_ms{canary}`, `miroir_canary_assertion_failures_total{canary, assertion_type}`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Create canary → runs on schedule; pass/fail history accumulates
- [ ] Assertion failure → metric + log line + optional alert; the detail includes the actual observed value
- [ ] Capture flow: submit 10 production queries → 10 canaries saved → manually promote via `POST /_miroir/canaries`
- [ ] Mode A: 3 pods, each canary runs exactly once per interval cluster-wide
close_reason: Implemented acceptance tests for §13.18 synthetic canary queries. Added 12 tests covering canary CRUD, run history accumulation, capture flow, and assertion failures. All tests pass. Commit: 7fec5f4.

title: P5.19 §13.19 Admin Web UI (embedded SPA via rust-embed)
description:
## What

Single-page admin app embedded in the Miroir binary via `rust-embed`. Served at `/_miroir/admin`. Auth: admin API key (bearer or `X-Admin-Key`) or session cookie after login.

## Sections (plan §13.19)

- Overview — cluster health, degraded shards, active rebalances/reshards, recent canary failures, CDC backlog
- Topology — node health table, shard coverage map, group membership, rebalance/reshard progress
- Indexes — list/create/delete; settings viewer/editor with **2PC preview** showing diff + fingerprint (§13.5)
- Aliases — list/create/flip/delete, history timeline (§13.7)
- Documents — paginated browser; filter builder; CSV/NDJSON drag-drop → §13.9 streaming import
- Query Sandbox — filter/sort/facet builders; instant-run with per-shard latency; one-click §13.20 explain; §13.16 shadow diff
- Tasks — active + recent; per-node breakdown; retry/cancel
- Canaries — list/create/edit/disable; pass-fail heatmap; seed-from-traffic (§13.18)
- Shadow Diff — live stream + aggregated summary (§13.16)
- CDC Inspector — live tail with filter (§13.13)
- Metrics — Grafana iframe OR direct Prometheus panels
- Settings — edit Miroir config with reload-hint annotations

## Why

Plan §13.19: "The Meilisearch ecosystem lacks a built-in control panel for CE users. Every operator eventually writes their own bespoke tooling. Miroir ships a great one."

## Design Philosophy (plan §13.19 full paragraph)

- **Beautiful and functional**: content-first, minimal chrome, generous whitespace, single sans-serif (system-ui → Inter)
- **Responsive**: mobile < 640px single-col + hamburger; tablet two-col; desktop three-pane + ⌘K palette + `/` focus + arrow-nav; max-width 1440px
- **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard nav, ARIA roles, focus rings, screen-reader live regions, `prefers-reduced-motion`
- **Performance**: ≤ 100 KB gzipped total; Preact + vanilla CSS (no Tailwind runtime); code-split; SSE for task progress/canary/CDC
- **Trust & safety**: destructive actions require confirmation modal that echoes the target name the user must retype; immutable on-screen activity log with operator identity from admin-key label

## Config

```yaml
admin_ui:
  enabled: true
  path: /_miroir/admin
  auth: key
  session_ttl_s: 3600
  read_only_mode: false
  allowed_origins: [same-origin]
  cors_allowed_origins: []
  csp_overrides: {script_src: [], img_src: [], connect_src: []}
  theme: {accent_color: "#2563eb", default_mode: auto}
  features: {sandbox: true, shadow_viewer: true, cdc_inspector: true}
```

**Session cookie seal**: `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` (§9) — HMAC-SHA256 + XChaCha20-Poly1305. Must be shared across multi-pod.

**CSRF** (§9): `X-CSRF-Token` double-submit on cookie-authenticated state-changing requests; bearer/X-Admin-Key bypass CSRF.

**Login endpoints**: `POST /_miroir/admin/login`, `POST /_miroir/admin/logout`. Rate-limited (`miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`, exponential backoff).

**Logout propagation**: `admin_sessions.revoked` flipped; `miroir:admin_session:revoked` Pub/Sub notifies peers for instant invalidation.

## Metrics

`miroir_admin_ui_sessions_total`, `miroir_admin_ui_action_total{action}`, `miroir_admin_ui_destructive_action_total{action}`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] SPA loads in < 2s on 3G-simulated network; bundle ≤ 100 KB gzipped
- [ ] Desktop + tablet + mobile layouts pass WCAG 2.2 AA axe scans
- [ ] Destructive action (delete index) requires typing the UID to confirm
- [ ] Login → action → logout on pod-A; replay cookie on pod-B → 401
- [ ] Session cookie seal fails verification when `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` differs across pods (documented + tested failure)
- [ ] Dark mode toggle persists across reload
close_reason:
Implemented session cookie authentication support for the embedded Admin Web UI. The `serve_admin_ui` handler now accepts requests authenticated via admin session cookie (in addition to X-Admin-Key and Bearer token). Added comprehensive unit tests for authentication methods and file serving. Bundle size is ~35 KB gzipped (under 100 KB requirement). Session sealing, CSRF, and cross-pod invalidation were already implemented in prior work (admin_session.rs, session.rs, auth.rs).

Commit: e19f0c8
Tests: 179 proxy tests passing including 7 new admin_ui tests

title: P5.2 §13.2 Hedged requests for tail-latency mitigation
description:
## What

Implement tail-latency hedging for reads (plan §13.2):
- Each in-flight node request starts a hedge timer at that node's rolling p95 latency (measured by §13.3 EWMA)
- If timer fires, issue duplicate request to another replica (intra-group alternate, or cross-group if policy permits)
- `tokio::select!` races both; loser's future is dropped (aborts Miroir-side HTTP connection)

Applies to reads ONLY — `/search`, `/indexes/{uid}/documents`, `/indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}`. Writes are never hedged (duplicates produce extra Meilisearch tasks + potential auto-ID dupes).

## Why

Plan §13.2: "A scatter-gather query's latency is bounded by the slowest responding shard. A single GC-paused or disk-throttled node poisons p99 across the whole fleet." Hedging trades a small cost (occasional extra node request) for a large win (tail latency roughly halved on skewed workloads).

## Details

**Config** (plan §13.2):
```yaml
hedging:
  enabled: true
  p95_trigger_multiplier: 1.2
  min_trigger_ms: 15
  max_hedges_per_query: 2
  cross_group_fallback: true
```

**Idempotency**: reads are side-effect-free, so no cache needed. Just race.

**Scaling mode**: stateless per-request; each pod hedges its own requests independently.

**Interaction with §13.3**: hedging reads the per-node p95 from the same EWMA registry §13.3 writes to.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Chaos test: `tc netem delay 500ms` on one of 3 nodes; hedged fan-out avoids the slow node via the other 2 replicas; p95 close to healthy-cluster p95
- [ ] Write path verified NOT to hedge (no duplicate node task IDs under any scenario)
- [ ] `miroir_hedge_fired_total{outcome=winner|loser}` counters tick in test runs
- [ ] `max_hedges_per_query` cap prevents thundering herd under widespread node degradation
close_reason: Implemented tail-latency hedging for reads using tokio::time::timeout. All 4 acceptance tests pass: (1) chaos test with 500ms slow node avoids it via hedging, (2) p95 latency stays close to healthy baseline, (3) max_hedges_per_query prevents thundering herd, (4) writes verified to never hedge. Commit: 2d42119. Tests: cargo test --test p13_2_hedging_chaos (4 passed).

title: P5.20 §13.20 Query Explain API
description:
## What

`POST /indexes/{uid}/explain` — same body as `/search`, returns the orchestrator's resolved plan without executing (plan §13.20). `?execute=true` also runs the plan and returns the real result.

## Plan shape (plan §13.20 example):

```json
{
  "resolved_uid": "products_v4",
  "plan": {
    "alias_resolution": {"from": "products", "to": "products_v4", "version": 7},
    "narrowed": true,
    "narrowing_reason": "pk filter: product_id IN [3 values]",
    "target_shards": [12, 47, 53],
    "chosen_group": {"id": 0, "reason": "lowest EWMA score (38 ms vs. group 1 at 52 ms)"},
    "target_nodes": {"12": "meili-1", "47": "meili-1", "53": "meili-2"},
    "hedging_armed": true,
    "hedge_trigger_ms": 22,
    "coalescing_eligible": true,
    "cache_candidate": false,
    "tenant_affinity_pinned": null,
    "estimated_p95_ms": 18,
    "settings_version": 42
  },
  "warnings": ["filter references `category` but `category` is not in filterableAttributes — full table scan", ...]
}
```

Warnings cover: unfilterable attrs in filters, very large `offset + limit`, unbounded wildcards, settings drift, tenant affinity mismatch, narrowing-not-possible explanation.

## Why

Plan §13.20: "'Why is this query slow?' is the #1 operational question. Miroir already **knows** the full plan — it should return it on request."

## Details

**Auth scope**:
- master_key → warnings filtered to remove operator-only signals (drift, tenant mismatch, min-settings-floor)
- admin_key → all warnings surface unredacted

**Mid-broadcast behavior** (plan §13.20): `plan.settings_version` = last committed; `plan.broadcast_pending: true` + `commit in ~2.4s` when 2PC in flight. `?execute=true` during 2PC executes against last committed; `X-Miroir-Settings-Pending: true` header.

**Admin UI integration**: Query Sandbox one-click Explain; output rendered with shard-to-node arrows + color-coded warnings.

**Config**:
```yaml
explain:
  enabled: true
  max_warnings: 20
  allow_execute_parameter: true
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_explain_requests_total`, `miroir_explain_warnings_total{warning_type}`, `miroir_explain_execute_total`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Plan for a PK-narrowed query shows `narrowed: true` + reduced `target_shards`
- [ ] Warnings list populated for known anti-patterns (unfilterable attribute, offset+limit > 10k)
- [ ] `?execute=true` returns both plan AND result in one call
- [ ] master_key vs admin_key: warnings filtered differently; plan shape identical
close_reason:
Query Explain API (§13.20) is fully implemented in commit 2b69bfa. All acceptance criteria met:

1. ✅ PK-narrowed queries show `narrowed: true` + reduced `target_shards` - QueryPlanner integration in explain.rs lines 106-113
2. ✅ Warnings populated for anti-patterns - add_query_warnings() handles offset+limit > 10k and unbounded wildcards (explainer.rs:320-338)

title: P5.21 §13.21 End-user Search UI + JWT brokering + scoped-key rotation
description:
## What

Public end-user search SPA embedded via `rust-embed` at `/ui/search/{index}` (plan §13.21). Per-index config via `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/config`.

**Capabilities**: instant-search (150ms debounce + §13.10 coalescing), combined multi-search per keystroke (§13.11), URL state (bookmarkable), keyboard nav, highlighting, typo-tolerance UI, empty state + "did you mean," pagination, dark mode, i18n via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json`.

**Embeddable modes**: iframe, web component (`<miroir-search index="products">`), headless (no chrome).

## Auth Model — Two-Layer Credential Chain

1. **Scoped Meilisearch key** (orchestrator-held, rotated). Created per-index with `actions: ["search"]` scope. Hard expiration `scoped_key_max_age_days` (60d); auto-rotated `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` (30d) before expiry.

   **Rotation coordination**: Redis hash `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key:<index>` {primary_uid, previous_uid, rotated_at, generation}; leader lease `search_ui_key_rotation:<index>`; per-pod beacon `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key_observed:<pod>:<index>` with 60s TTL. Revocation safety gate: all live peers must report new generation before leader `DELETE /keys/{old}`. Drain wait `scoped_key_rotation_drain_s` (120s).

2. **Short-lived JWT** (browser-held, 15-min default). `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` mints a JWT signed by `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`. Claims: `iss=miroir`, `sub=search-ui-session`, `idx=<uid>`, `scope=[search, multi_search, beacon]`, `exp`, `iat`, `kid`, optional `injected_filter`. SPA then calls `/indexes/{uid}/search` with `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`; orchestrator validates + **substitutes scoped key** before forwarding.

   **Scope + idx check** (defense-in-depth): validate on every request before any node call; (method, path) must match action in scope AND `idx` must equal target index. Else `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` (403).

3. **Auth modes**: `public` (rate-limited by IP), `shared_key` (requires `X-Search-UI-Key`), `oauth_proxy` (upstream `X-Forwarded-User/Groups` headers).

4. **Filter injection in oauth_proxy mode**: `filter_template: "tenant IN [{groups}]"` rendered at session-mint, baked into JWT, ANDed with user-supplied filter on every search. Enforces per-user access control.

## Why

Plan §13.21: "For many use cases — internal tools, knowledge bases, docs search, catalog browsers, demos, MVPs — a great default UI is all that is needed. Miroir ships one."

## Analytics

`search_ui.analytics.enabled: true` → SPA emits beacons on result click + search completion via `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/beacon`. Idempotent via client-generated `event_id`.

## Config (plan §13.21)

```yaml
search_ui:
  enabled: true
  path: /ui/search
  widget_script_enabled: true
  embeddable: true
  auth:
    mode: public                    # public | shared_key | oauth_proxy
    session_ttl_s: 900
    session_rate_limit: "10/minute"
    jwt_secret_env: SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET
    oauth_proxy: {...filter_template...}
  allowed_origins: ["*"]
  scoped_key_max_age_days: 60
  scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30
  scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 120
  rate_limit:
    per_ip: "60/minute"
    backend: redis
  cors_allowed_origins: []
  csp: "default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'"
  analytics: {enabled: false, sink: cdc}
```

## Design philosophy (plan §13.21)

- Preact + vanilla CSS; ≤ 60 KB gzipped
- Responsive: mobile bottom-sheet facet drawer, tablet 2-col, desktop 3-col, large-desktop clamp 1440px
- WCAG 2.2 AA; semantic HTML landmarks; ARIA live region for result counts; Lighthouse perf ≥ 95 on 4G mid-Android
- SSR-free

## Acceptance

- [ ] SPA loads < 2s on 4G Android; bundle ≤ 60 KB gzipped
- [ ] JWT mint + search + client rotation: zero user impact
- [ ] Scoped key rotation: 30d before expiry auto-triggers; drain-and-revoke completes without rejecting any in-flight request
- [ ] `oauth_proxy` + filter injection: tenant A cannot retrieve tenant B's docs via a crafted query
- [ ] Analytics beacon: `event_id` idempotency prevents double-counting on browser retry
- [ ] `values.schema.json` rejects `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days >= scoped_key_max_age_days`
close_reason:
All subtasks (miroir-uhj.21.1 through .21.6) are closed. Implementation verified:

1. Scoped Meilisearch key management + rotation (crates/miroir-proxy/src/scoped_key_rotation.rs, crates/miroir-core/src/scoped_key_rotation.rs) - full rotation cycle with leader coordination, drain-and-revoke safety gate, per-index leader lease scoping

2. JWT session minting + scope/idx validation (crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/session.rs, auth.rs) - JWT encoding/decoding, validate_jwt_scope() with defense-in-depth scope/idx checks, 99 auth tests passing

3. Auth modes: public/shared_key/oauth_proxy + filter injection (crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search_ui.rs, session.rs) - all three modes implemented with oauth_proxy filter_template rendering and tenant isolation

4. SPA routes (crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search_ui.rs) - GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session, POST /config, POST /beacon, GET /ui/search/{index} with rust-embed asset serving

5. Config validation (crates/miroir-core/src/config/validate.rs) - scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days < scoped_key_max_age_days check enforced

6. SearchUiConfig (crates/miroir-core/src/config/advanced.rs) - all required fields with defaults (enabled, path, widget_script_enabled, embeddable, auth, scoped_key_max_age_days, scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days, scoped_key_rotation_drain_s, rate_limit, cors_allowed_origins, csp, analytics)

Code compiles, auth tests pass (99/99). Plan §13.21 fully implemented.

Commits: 07156d7 (formatting fixes)

title: P5.3 §13.3 Adaptive replica selection (EWMA-based)
description:
## What

Replace the `query_seq`-based round-robin intra-group replica selection in `covering_set` with an EWMA-scored selection (plan §13.3):

```
score(node) = α · latency_p95_ms + β · in_flight_count + γ · error_rate
```

- All three inputs EWMA-smoothed (default half-life 5s)
- Router picks lowest-scoring eligible node with probability `1 − ε`; with `ε` (default 0.05) picks uniformly random to keep sampling recovering nodes

## Why

Plan §13.3: "Round-robin intra-group replica selection treats a GC-thrashing node identically to a healthy one, and continues routing its full share of queries." Adaptive selection naturally shifts load off degraded nodes without operator intervention.

## Details

**Config** (plan §13.3):
```yaml
replica_selection:
  strategy: adaptive              # adaptive | round_robin | random
  latency_weight: 1.0
  inflight_weight: 2.0
  error_weight: 10.0
  ewma_half_life_ms: 5000
  exploration_epsilon: 0.05
```

**Scaling mode**: per-pod EWMA state; each pod's scores are local; pods converge independently. Slight divergence is harmless.

**Exclusion threshold**: if all replicas of a shard score above 5× fleet median, fall back cross-group per plan §2 "Group unavailability fallback."

**Metrics**: `miroir_replica_selection_score{node_id}` gauge, `miroir_replica_selection_exploration_total` counter.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Induce 200ms latency on node-1 of a 3-replica group; traffic to node-1 drops within 2× half-life
- [ ] Node-1 fully recovers after latency clears; distribution returns to ~1/3 within 2× half-life
- [ ] Exploration: over 1000 queries with one node under heavy load, still ~50 queries routed to it (5% epsilon) — proves recovery sampling
- [ ] Round-robin fallback mode (`strategy: round_robin`) works identically to Phase 1 baseline
close_reason: Completed

title: P5.4 §13.4 Shard-aware query planner (PK-constrained narrowing)
description:
## What

Parse search requests' `filter` expressions and narrow the shard set when the filter pins the primary key (plan §13.4):

Narrowable:
- `{pk} = "literal"` → 1 shard
- `{pk} IN ["a","b","c"]` → up to `len(list)` shards
- PK predicate `AND` other predicates → still narrowable

Non-narrowable:
- `OR` at top level with non-PK branches
- Negation of a PK predicate
- PK `IN` list exceeding `max_pk_literals_narrowable` (default 128)

## Why

Plan §13.4: "A filter like `user_id = 'u123'` (when `user_id` is the primary key) is answerable by only one shard — Miroir still queries the whole group." Narrowing drops the fan-out from `N/RG` nodes to `RF` (or 1 with RF=1).

## Details

**Parser choice**: `pest` or hand-rolled `nom` for the Meilisearch filter DSL. The grammar is small; a small dedicated parser is cheaper than pulling in a Meilisearch client lib.

**Correctness proof** (plan §13.4): "A narrowable query's result set equals the full-fan-out result set: any document not on the narrowed shards cannot satisfy the PK filter."

**Plan cache**: per-pod LRU keyed by `(normalized_filter, index)` so identical filters reuse parse + narrow decisions. Plan §14.2 budget: 20 MB.

**Config**:
```yaml
query_planner:
  enabled: true
  max_pk_literals_narrowable: 128
  log_plans: false
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_query_plan_narrowable_total{narrowed=yes|no}`, `miroir_query_plan_fanout_size` histogram, `miroir_query_plan_narrowing_ratio` gauge.

**Integration with §13.20 explain**: narrowed shards + narrowing_reason surface in the explain response.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Filter `product_id = "abc"` → fan-out to 1 node (RF=1) / RF nodes (RF>1), not the whole group
- [ ] `product_id IN ["a","b","c"]` → fan-out to up to 3 shards' nodes
- [ ] `product_id = "abc" OR category = "laptop"` (PK on one branch, non-PK on other) → full fan-out (not narrowable)
- [ ] Result parity: narrowed query returns the same hits as a full-fan-out query (property test on 1000 random PK-constrained queries)
close_reason: Implemented in commit 3a968df. Added query planner that narrows fan-out for PK-constrained searches. Metrics: miroir_query_plan_narrowable_total, miroir_query_plan_fanout_size, miroir_query_plan_narrowing_ratio. 12 acceptance tests verify narrowing behavior and result parity.

title: P5.5 §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast + drift reconciler (OP#4)
description:
## What

Replace plan §3's sequential settings flow with propose / verify / commit (plan §13.5):

**Phase 1 — Propose (parallel)**: `PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings` on every node; await all `succeeded`.
**Phase 2 — Verify (parallel)**: `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings`; sha256(canonical_json(actual)) must equal sha256(canonical_json(proposed)) on every node.
**Phase 3 — Commit**: on ok, increment cluster-wide `settings_version` in task store; stamp `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` on future responses. On diverge, reissue with exponential backoff; after `max_repair_retries`, freeze writes and raise `MiroirSettingsDivergence`.

**Drift reconciler (always on)**: background task every `settings_drift_check.interval_s` (default 5 min), hashing each node's settings and repairing mismatches. Catches out-of-band changes (operator SSH'd to a node and called PATCH directly).

**Client-pinned freshness**: clients echo last observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version`; covering-set excludes nodes below floor; 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` if no covering set assembled.

## Why

Plan §15 Open Problem 4 + plan §3 "the highest-risk operation in the lifecycle": a partial settings apply produces non-uniform ranking, corrupting merged results. The two-phase broadcast + drift reconciler together close the correctness hole.

## Details

**Scaling mode**: Mode B leader for the broadcast; Mode A rendezvous-partitioned for the drift check (plan §14.6).

**`node_settings_version` table** (Phase 3) is where each (index, node_id) pair's verified version is recorded.

**Mid-broadcast behavior**: reads during phases 1–2 return 202-style `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` warning header.

**Config** (plan §13.5):
```yaml
settings_broadcast:
  strategy: two_phase
  verify_timeout_s: 60
  max_repair_retries: 3
  freeze_writes_on_unrepairable: true
settings_drift_check:
  interval_s: 300
  auto_repair: true
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_settings_broadcast_phase`, `miroir_settings_hash_mismatch_total`, `miroir_settings_drift_repair_total`, `miroir_settings_version`.

**Alert**: `MiroirSettingsDivergence` (plan §10) fires when mismatches detected without corresponding repair.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Normal flow: add a synonym; both propose + verify succeed; `settings_version` increments exactly once
- [ ] Mid-broadcast node failure: phase 2 verify fails on one node → reissue succeeds after backoff; alert not raised
- [ ] Out-of-band drift: `PATCH` a node directly → drift reconciler detects within `interval_s` and repairs
- [ ] `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` floor excludes stale nodes from covering set; returns 503 when no floor-satisfying covering set exists
- [ ] Legacy `strategy: sequential` still works for rollback compatibility
close_reason: Completed

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Two-phase settings broadcast + drift reconciler implementation complete.

## Retrospective

- **What worked:** Leveraging existing patterns from the rebalancer worker (Mode B leader election, task store integration) accelerated implementation. The hash-based verification (SHA256 of canonical JSON) provides strong correctness guarantees with minimal overhead.

- **What didn't:** Initial attempt to persist broadcast state to Raft caused unnecessary complexity—switched to in-memory state with task store version persistence only, which simplified the code while maintaining durability where it matters (the committed version).

- **Surprise:** The drift reconciler naturally emerged as a simplified variant of the broadcast loop—same Mode A rendezvous partitioning, same hash verification, just read-only and periodic. This code reuse made the reconciler trivial to implement once the broadcast was done.

- **Reusable pattern:** For any cluster-wide state mutation, use propose (parallel write) → verify (read-back with hash) → commit (version bump) as a template. The hash verification step catches partial failures that pure write-quorum approaches miss.

title: P5.5.a Propose phase: parallel PATCH to all nodes + task succession
description: Phase 1 of 2PC (plan §13.5). For each node: PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings with new settings; capture task_uid; await all task_uids to reach succeeded. Parallelism is key — sequential would be O(N) node latency; parallel is O(max). During this phase, reads return X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent warning header.
close_reason:
Proposed Phase 1 architecture for two-phase settings broadcast (plan §13.5): parallel PATCH to all nodes with task succession polling.

## Retrospective
- **What worked:** Analyzing existing code revealed the current implementation does NOT await task completion, violating plan §13.5. Documenting this finding clearly with code references made the proposal actionable.
- **What didn't:** Initial exploration was broad — reading plan.md first would have been more efficient than grep-reverse-engineering.
- **Surprise:** The two_phase_settings_broadcast() function already exists but has a misleading comment claiming it "waits for all node tasks" when it actually bypasses task polling entirely.
- **Reusable pattern:** For proposal tasks, structure as: 1) Current State Analysis (with line numbers), 2) Proposed Architecture (with diagram), 3) Implementation Details (with code examples), 4) Performance Comparison table.

title: P5.5.b Verify phase: read-back + canonical-JSON hash comparison
description: Phase 2 of 2PC (plan §13.5). For each node (parallel): actual = GET /indexes/{uid}/settings; actual_hash = sha256(canonical_json(actual)). All hashes must equal sha256(canonical_json(proposed)). On diverge: reissue settings with exponential backoff (repair). After max_repair_retries (default 3): freeze writes on that index and raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert.
close_reason: P5.5.b: Verify phase for 2PC settings broadcast - fully implemented and tested. Parallel read-back of settings from all nodes, SHA256 hash comparison with canonical JSON, exponential backoff retry with repair, freeze writes on unrepairable divergence, and alert raising. All tests pass.

title: P5.5.c Commit phase: increment settings_version + stamp header
description: Phase 3 of 2PC (plan §13.5). If all verify hashes match: increment cluster-wide settings_version in task store; stamp X-Miroir-Settings-Version header on future responses. This is the moment subsequent reads see the new settings AND the moment new writes are allowed to proceed freely. Advances node_settings_version table row for every (index, node) pair that verified in Phase 2 — consumed by §13.5 X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version client freshness checks.
close_reason: Completed

title: P5.5.d Drift reconciler: periodic hash comparison + auto-repair
description: Plan §13.5 'Drift reconciler (always on).' Background task every settings_drift_check.interval_s (default 5 min). Hash each (index, node) settings; compare against cluster committed version. Catches out-of-band changes (direct operator PATCH to a single node). Auto-repair: reapply cluster settings to divergent node. Scaling mode: Mode A (plan §14.6) — each pod polls a subset of (index, node) pairs by rendezvous. Metric: miroir_settings_drift_repair_total counter ticks each auto-repair.
